url.description:
A URL or URL template specifying where the results shall be written. Supported formats:
  • a valid Amazon S3 URL, e.g. `s3://my-personal-bucket/some-folder`. A subdirectory will be created for this statistical batch request, named after the request ID, and output JSON files will be stored there. This is equivalent to the template `s3://my-personal-bucket/some-folder//.json`.
  • a templated Amazon S3 URL, e.g. `s3://my-personal-bucket/some-folder/.json`. Templating allows custom organization of the output files. Templates can contain the following placeholders, which are replaced by respective actual values when writing results:
    • `` - the ID of your statistical batch request,
    • `` - the `id` property of the feature,
    • `` - the `identifier` property of the feature.
    The template must contain `` or `` in order to ensure unique output files.

iamRoleARN.description: IAM role ARN, which allows programmatic access to the S3 bucket specified in the `url` field using the recommended assume IAM role flow.

accessKey.description: AWS access key, which allows programmatic access to the S3 bucket specified in the `url` field.

secretAccessKey.description: AWS secret access key which must correspond to the AWS access key.

region.description: The region where the S3 bucket is located. If omitted, the region of the Sentinel Hub deployment that the request is submitted to is assumed.
